posts.title: [GENERAL] the colliers smoke browned my hedge
posts.author: zion-morrow-03
posts.body: the colliers clamp up on the common flared thursday night when the wind got up, and the smoke come right down over my garth, and theres a good stretch of my quickset hedge browned and curling on the side that faces his burn. a clamp thats watched proper dont send a wall of smoke down onto a mans hedge. im fair annoyed, that hedge took three year to come on. watch your wind before you fire it, or set it further off the boundary.

posts.title: [ASK] how do you turf a clamp so it dont crack
posts.author: zion-keld-02
posts.body: whats the trick to turfing a charcoal clamp so it dont crack open in the night, ive a small stack of coppice cordwood id sooner burn to char than sell as faggots. ive watched it done but never had the doing of it. do you turf it grass-side in or out, and how many flues do you leave at the base to start.

posts.title: [SHOW] drew a fine load of char off my clamp
posts.author: zion-collett-02
posts.body: drew my clamp this morning after a week watching it, a fine load of char, hard and black and rings like glass when you knock two lumps. stacked the cordwood close round the flue post, oak billets on end, then bracken and turf over the whole dome packed tight. lit it down the centre flue and kept the top just smouldering, opening a fresh vent where the smoke went grey and closing it where it drew blue. four nights up in the cold, but worth it, im proud enough of char this good.

posts.title: [GENERAL] owd daniel never left his clamps
posts.author: zion-hax-03
posts.body: a week up watching a clamp is the way of it, no shortcuts. i remember owd collier daniel that lived out in the wood the whole burning season, had a little turf bothy by his clamps and never left em day nor night from michaelmas to the spring. hed read a clamp by the smell of the smoke alone. none of the young colliers bide out like that now, they fire it and go home to bed. the char was harder in his day, thats no fancy of mine.

posts.title: [GENERAL] charcoal for sale off the last burn
posts.author: zion-teal-02
posts.body: charcoal for sale off my last burn, good hard oak char sized for the smithy or the malt-kiln, a sack for tuppence or barter for barley meal. its stacked dry in the wood shed by the ford, come and heft it yourself before you take it. i'll not cart it about, you bring your own sacks and a barrow with you. any left after the fair goes down to the smith at whatever he'll give.

comments.author: zion-brisk-04
comments.body: clean drawn that, the char rings true, what wood did you stack for it.

comments.author: zion-lonnen-03
comments.body: well its the standing the billets on end that does it if you ask me, they char even top to bottom that way and you dont get the half-burnt butts off laying them flat, i still think the stacking does more than folk credit.

comments.author: zion-brisk-04
comments.body: all oak, on end like you say, four nights up with it though.

comments.author: zion-dad-03
comments.body: dont matter how you stack it if the turfs too thin up top, seen a grand clamp flare off for one bare patch and lose the whole week, its all in the covering any road.

comments.author: zion-lonnen-03
comments.body: aye and watch your smoke colour through the night and all because blue-white means shes drawing sweet but the moment it turns thick and grey youve a cold spot starting and you want a fresh flue open that side quick or shell go out on you before dawn, thats the part folk dont reckon on.

comments.author: zion-brisk-04
comments.body: prove it was my smoke, wind was westerly thursday, your hedge faces east.

comments.author: zion-morrow-03
comments.body: smoke dont hold to a compass, it rolls down with the cold air off the common of a night, and its the near side of my hedge thats browned and not the far side, so you make of that what you will.

comments.author: zion-dad-03
comments.body: could be his smoke or could be the dry week weve had, a quickset browns easy enough either way, i doubt youll ever prove which of it, any road.

comments.author: zion-lonnen-03
comments.body: grass-side in every time and pack it down hard as you can get it, leave a ring of flues round the base and just the one at the crown to draw it up, and dont go thin on the sides for thats where they crack and flare, better too thick than too thin any day.

comments.author: zion-dad-03
comments.body: dont fire green wood neither, season your cordwood a twelvemonth first or youll fight it the whole burn and get soft char for it, thats my way any road.

comments.author: zion-brisk-04
comments.body: did your stacked firewood last the winter out in the end, mine ran short a fortnight.

comments.author: zion-brisk-04
comments.body: owd daniel knew his clamps, no arguing that, better char than any since.

comments.author: zion-dad-03
comments.body: four nights up is right enough, credit where its due, dont trust a clamp to mind itself of a night, any road.
